mythril.source_mode_result: blocked: Mythril attempted to fetch solc from solc-bin.ethereum.org and DNS resolution failed
mythril.bytecode_mode_result: completed with generic bytecode-level warnings; no actionable exploit path confirmed

findings.title: Slither flags migration balance reads before external calls
findings.description: Slither reports that migrate reads token balances and later makes external token and position-manager calls.
findings.triage: The function is operator-only, protected by nonReentrant, and records migrated state before external calls. No practical exploit path was confirmed.
findings.recommendation: Keep the current ordering and guard. Add a malicious-token or malicious-position-manager regression test before suppressing the warning permanently.

findings.title: Example deploy script has unused constants
findings.description: Two constants are declared but not used.
findings.triage: This is not a contract vulnerability, but can confuse operators if the values look authoritative.
findings.recommendation: Remove the constants or wire them into script validation.

findings.title: Mythril bytecode mode produced generic arithmetic warnings
findings.description: Mythril bytecode mode reported integer arithmetic warnings across multiple contracts.
findings.triage: The code compiles with Solidity 0.8.26 checked arithmetic, uses explicit bounds and FullMath where needed, and passed Foundry tests and invariants. No source-level exploit path was confirmed.
findings.recommendation: Re-run Mythril source-mode with a locally available pinned Solidity compiler.

findings.title: Mythril fallback requirement warning
findings.description: Mythril reported a generic fallback requirement warning in bytecode mode.
findings.triage: Contracts that should reject native ETH do so explicitly, and no fund-loss or authorization-bypass path was confirmed.
findings.recommendation: Keep explicit ETH rejection where native ETH should not be accepted. Re-run Mythril source-mode before mainnet.

bottom_line: No confirmed critical or high-severity exploitable vulnerability was found in the project-owned Autolaunch contracts during this pass. The main remaining release risk is deployment verification: canonical USDC and accepted ownership must be proven across the full launch stack.
